NKJV.1: And again He entered Capernaum after some days, and it was heard that He was in the house.
NKJV.2: Immediately many gathered together, so that there was no longer room to receive them, not even near the door. And He preached the word to them.
NKJV.3: Then they came to Him, bringing a paralytic who was carried by four men.
NKJV.4: And when they could not come near Him because of the crowd, they uncovered the roof where He was. So when they had broken through, they let down the bed on which the paralytic was lying.
NKJV.5: When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven you.”
NKJV.6: And some of the scribes were sitting there and reasoning in their hearts,
NKJV.7: “Why does this Man speak blasphemies like this? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
NKJV.8: But immediately, when Jesus perceived in His spirit that they reasoned thus within themselves, He said to them, “Why do you reason about these things in your hearts?
NKJV.9: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven you,’ or to say, ‘Arise, take up your bed and walk’?
NKJV.10: But that you may know that the Son of Man has power on earth to forgive sins”—He said to the paralytic,
NKJV.11: “I say to you, arise, take up your bed, and go to your house.”
NKJV.12: Immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went out in the presence of them all, so that all were amazed and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”
NKJV.13: Then He went out again by the sea; and all the multitude came to Him, and He taught them.
NKJV.14: As He passed by, He saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax office. And He said to him, “Follow Me.” So he arose and followed Him.
NKJV.15: Now it happened, as He was dining in Levi’s house, that many tax collectors and sinners also sat together with Jesus and His disciples; for there were many, and they followed Him.
NKJV.16: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw Him eating with the tax collectors and sinners, they said to His disciples, “How is it that He eats and drinks with tax collectors and sinners?”
NKJV.17: When Jesus heard it, He said to them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick. I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners, to repentance.”
NKJV.18: The disciples of John and of the Pharisees were fasting. Then they came and said to Him, “Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but Your disciples do not fast?”
NKJV.19: And Jesus said to them, “Can the friends of the bridegroom fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them they cannot fast.
NKJV.20: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be  taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days.
NKJV.21: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; or else the new piece pulls away from the old, and the tear is made worse.
NKJV.22: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; or else the new wine bursts the wineskins, the wine is spilled, and the wineskins are ruined. But new wine must be put into new wineskins.”
NKJV.23: Now it happened that He went through the grainfields on the Sabbath; and as they went His disciples began to pluck the heads of grain.
NKJV.24: And the Pharisees said to Him, “Look, why do they do what is not lawful on the Sabbath?”
NKJV.25: But He said to them, “Have you never read  what David did when he was in need and hungry, he and those with him:
NKJV.26: how he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the showbread, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and also gave some to those who were with him?”
NKJV.27: And He said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, and not man for the  Sabbath.
NKJV.28: Therefore  the Son of Man is also Lord of the Sabbath.”

ESV.1: And when he returned to Capernaum after some days, it was reported that he was at home.
ESV.2: And many were gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even at the door. And he was preaching the word to them.
ESV.3: And they came, bringing to him a paralytic carried by four men.
ESV.4: And when they could not get near him because of the crowd, they removed the roof above him, and when they had made an opening, they let down the bed on which the paralytic lay.
ESV.5: And when Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
ESV.6: Now some of the scribes were sitting there, questioning in their hearts,
ESV.7: “Why does this man speak like that? He is blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
ESV.8: And immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they thus questioned within themselves, said to them, “Why do you question these things in your hearts?
ESV.9: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Rise, take up your bed and walk’?
ESV.10: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he said to the paralytic —
ESV.11: “I say to you, rise, pick up your bed, and go home.”
ESV.12: And he rose and immediately picked up his bed and went out before them all, so that they were all amazed and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”
ESV.13: He went out again beside the sea, and all the crowd was coming to him, and he was teaching them.
ESV.14: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he rose and followed him.
ESV.15: And as he reclined at table in his house, many tax collectors and sinners were reclining with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many who followed him.
ESV.16: And the scribes of  the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, said to his disciples, “Why does he eat  with tax collectors and sinners?”
ESV.17: And when Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.”
ESV.18: Now John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. And people came and said to him, “Why do John's disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
ESV.19: And Jesus said to them,  “Can the wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
ESV.20: The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast in that day.
ESV.21: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. If he does, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and a worse tear is made.
ESV.22: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the wine will burst the skins — and the wine is destroyed, and so are the skins. But new wine is for fresh wineskins.”
ESV.23: One Sabbath he was going through the grainfields, and as they made their way, his disciples began to pluck heads of grain.
ESV.24: And the Pharisees were saying to him, “Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?”
ESV.25: And he said to them,  “Have you never read what David did, when he was in need and was hungry, he and those who were with him:
ESV.26: how he entered the house of God, in the time of  Abiathar the high priest, and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and also gave it to those who were with him?”
ESV.27: And he said to them,  “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
ESV.28: So the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”

NASB.1: When He had come back to Capernaum several days afterward, it was heard that He was at home.
NASB.2: And many were gathered together, so that there was no longer room, not even near the door; and He was speaking the word to them.
NASB.3: And they came, bringing to Him a paralytic, carried by four men.
NASB.4: Being unable to get to Him because of the crowd, they removed the roof above Him; and when they had dug an opening, they let down the pallet on which the paralytic was lying.
NASB.5: And Jesus seeing their faith said to the paralytic, 'Son, your sins are forgiven.'
NASB.6: But some of the scribes were sitting there and reasoning in their hearts,
NASB.7: 'Why does this man speak that way? He is blaspheming; who can forgive sins but God alone?'
NASB.8: Immediately Jesus, aware in His spirit that they were reasoning that way within themselves, said to them, 'Why are you reasoning about these things in your hearts?
NASB.9: 'Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, 'Your sins are forgiven'; or to say, 'Get up, and pick up your pallet and walk '?
NASB.10: 'But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins '-- He said to the paralytic,
NASB.11: 'I say to you, get up, pick up your pallet and go home.'
NASB.12: And he got up and immediately picked up the pallet and went out in the sight of everyone, so that they were all amazed and were glorifying God, saying, 'We have never seen anything like this.'
NASB.13: And He went out again by the seashore; and all the people were coming to Him, and He was teaching them.
NASB.14: As He passed by, He saw Levi the [son] of Alphaeus sitting in the tax booth, and He said to him, 'Follow Me!' And he got up and followed Him.
NASB.15: And it happened that He was reclining [at the table] in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners were dining with Jesus and His disciples; for there were many of them, and they were following Him.
NASB.16: When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that He was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, they said to His disciples, 'Why is He eating and drinking with tax collectors and sinners?'
NASB.17: And hearing [this], Jesus said to them, '[It is] not those who are healthy who need a physician, but those who are sick; I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners.'
NASB.18: John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting; and they came and said to Him, 'Why do John's disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but Your disciples do not fast?'
NASB.19: And Jesus said to them, 'While the bridegroom is with them, the attendants of the bridegroom cannot fast, can they? So long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
NASB.20: 'But the days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast in that day.
NASB.21: 'No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; otherwise the patch pulls away from it, the new from the old, and a worse tear results.
NASB.22: 'No one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost and the skins [as well]; but [one puts] new wine into fresh wineskins.'
NASB.23: And it happened that He was passing through the grainfields on the Sabbath, and His disciples began to make their way along while picking the heads [of grain].
NASB.24: The Pharisees were saying to Him, 'Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?'
NASB.25: And He said to them, 'Have you never read what David did when he was in need and he and his companions became hungry;
NASB.26: how he entered the house of God in the time of Abiathar [the] high priest, and ate the consecrated bread, which is not lawful for [anyone] to eat except the priests, and he also gave it to those who were with him?'
NASB.27: Jesus said to them, 'The Sabbath was made for man, and not man for the Sabbath.
NASB.28: 'So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.'

NLT.1: When Jesus returned to Capernaum several days later, the news spread quickly that he was back home.
NLT.2: Soon the house where he was staying was so packed with visitors that there was no more room, even outside the door. While he was preaching God’s word to them,
NLT.3: four men arrived carrying a paralyzed man on a mat.
NLT.4: They couldn’t bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, so they dug a hole through the roof above his head. Then they lowered the man on his mat, right down in front of Jesus.
NLT.5: Seeing their faith, Jesus said to the paralyzed man, “My child, your sins are forgiven.”
NLT.6: But some of the teachers of religious law who were sitting there thought to themselves,
NLT.7: “What is he saying? This is blasphemy! Only God can forgive sins!”
NLT.8: Jesus knew immediately what they were thinking, so he asked them, “Why do you question this in your hearts?
NLT.9: Is it easier to say to the paralyzed man ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or ‘Stand up, pick up your mat, and walk’?
NLT.10: So I will prove to you that the Son of Man has the authority on earth to forgive sins.” Then Jesus turned to the paralyzed man and said,
NLT.11: “Stand up, pick up your mat, and go home!”
NLT.12: And the man jumped up, grabbed his mat, and walked out through the stunned onlookers. They were all amazed and praised God, exclaiming, “We’ve never seen anything like this before!”
NLT.13: Then Jesus went out to the lakeshore again and taught the crowds that were coming to him.
NLT.14: As he walked along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at his tax collector’s booth. “Follow me and be my disciple,” Jesus said to him. So Levi got up and followed him.
NLT.15: Later, Levi invited Jesus and his disciples to his home as dinner guests, along with many tax collectors and other disreputable sinners. (There were many people of this kind among Jesus’ followers.)
NLT.16: But when the teachers of religious law who were Pharisees saw him eating with tax collectors and other sinners, they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with such scum? ”
NLT.17: When Jesus heard this, he told them, “Healthy people don’t need a doctor — sick people do. I have come to call not those who think they are righteous, but those who know they are sinners.”
NLT.18: Once when John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, some people came to Jesus and asked, “Why don’t your disciples fast like John’s disciples and the Pharisees do?”
NLT.19: Jesus replied, “Do wedding guests fast while celebrating with the groom? Of course not. They can’t fast while the groom is with them.
NLT.20: But someday the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
NLT.21: “Besides, who would patch old clothing with new cloth? For the new patch would shrink and rip away from the old cloth, leaving an even bigger tear than before.
NLT.22: “And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. For the wine would burst the wineskins, and the wine and the skins would both be lost. New wine calls for new wineskins.”
NLT.23: One Sabbath day as Jesus was walking through some grainfields, his disciples began breaking off heads of grain to eat.
NLT.24: But the Pharisees said to Jesus, “Look, why are they breaking the law by harvesting grain on the Sabbath?”
NLT.25: Jesus said to them, “Haven’t you ever read in the Scriptures what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
NLT.26: He went into the house of God (during the days when Abiathar was high priest) and broke the law by eating the sacred loaves of bread that only the priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his companions.”
NLT.27: Then Jesus said to them, “The Sabbath was made to meet the needs of people, and not people to meet the requirements of the Sabbath.
NLT.28: So the Son of Man is Lord, even over the Sabbath!”

WEB.1: When he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was in the house.
WEB.2: Immediately many were gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even around the door; and he spoke the word to them.
WEB.3: Four people came, carrying a paralytic to him.
WEB.4: When they could not come near to him for the crowd, they removed the roof where he was. When they had broken it up, they let down the mat that the paralytic was lying on.
WEB.5: Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven you.”
WEB.6: But there were some of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
WEB.7: “Why does this man speak blasphemies like that? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
WEB.8: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, said to them, “Why do you reason these things in your hearts?
WEB.9: Which is easier, to tell the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven;’ or to say, ‘Arise, and take up your bed, and walk?’
WEB.10: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he said to the paralytic —
WEB.11: “I tell you, arise, take up your mat, and go to your house.”
WEB.12: He arose, and immediately took up the mat, and went out in front of them all; so that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”
WEB.13: He went out again by the seaside. All the multitude came to him, and he taught them.
WEB.14: As he passed by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he arose and followed him.
WEB.15: He was reclining at the table in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners sat down with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him.
WEB.16: The scribes and the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, said to his disciples, “Why is it that he eats and drinks with tax collectors and sinners?”
WEB.17: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”
WEB.18: John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, and they came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples don’t fast?”
WEB.19: Jesus said to them, “Can the groomsmen fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they can’t fast.
WEB.20: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then will they fast in that day.
WEB.21: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, or else the patch shrinks and the new tears away from the old, and a worse hole is made.
WEB.22: No one puts new wine into old wineskins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and the wine pours out, and the skins will be destroyed; but they put new wine into fresh wineskins.”
WEB.23: He was going on the Sabbath day through the grain fields, and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of grain.
WEB.24: The Pharisees said to him, “Behold, why do they do that which is not lawful on the Sabbath day?”
WEB.25: He said to them, “Did you never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry — he, and those who were with him?
WEB.26: How he entered into God’s house when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the show bread, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and gave also to those who were with him?”
WEB.27: He said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
WEB.28: Therefore the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”

CSB17.1: When <sup>ⓕ</sup> he entered Capernaum <sup>ⓖ</sup> again after some days, it was reported that he was at home.
CSB17.2: So many people gathered together that there was no more room, not even in the doorway, and he was speaking the word <sup>ⓗ</sup> to them.
CSB17.3: They came to him bringing a paralytic, <sup>ⓘ</sup> carried by four of them.
CSB17.4: Since they were not able to bring him to <sup>[1]</sup> Jesus because of the crowd, they removed the roof above him, and after digging through it, they lowered the mat on which the paralytic was lying.
CSB17.5: Seeing their faith, <sup>ⓙ</sup> Jesus told the paralytic, “Son, your sins <sup>ⓚ</sup> are forgiven.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.6: But some of the scribes <sup>ⓜ</sup> were sitting there, questioning <sup>ⓝ</sup> in their hearts:
CSB17.7: “Why does he speak like this? He’s blaspheming! <sup>ⓐ</sup> Who can forgive <sup>ⓑ</sup> sins <sup>ⓒ</sup> but God alone?” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.8: Right away <sup>ⓔ</sup> Jesus perceived in his spirit <sup>ⓕ</sup> that they were thinking like this within themselves and said to them, “Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?
CSB17.9: Which is easier: <sup>ⓖ</sup> to say to the paralytic, <sup>ⓗ</sup> ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat, and walk’? <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.10: But so that you may know that the Son of Man <sup>ⓙ</sup> has authority <sup>ⓚ</sup> on earth to forgive sins” <sup>ⓛ</sup>  — he told the paralytic —
CSB17.11: “I tell you: get up, <sup>ⓜ</sup> take your mat, and go home.”
CSB17.12: Immediately he got up, took the mat, and went out in front of everyone. As a result, they were all astounded <sup>ⓝ</sup> and gave glory <sup>ⓐ</sup> to God, <sup>ⓑ</sup> saying, “We have never seen anything like this!” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.13: Jesus <sup>ⓓ</sup> went out again beside the sea. The whole crowd was coming to him, and he was teaching <sup>ⓔ</sup> them.
CSB17.14: Then, passing by, he saw Levi <sup>ⓕ</sup> the son of Alphaeus sitting at the toll booth, <sup>ⓖ</sup> and he said to him, “Follow me,” <sup>ⓗ</sup> and he got up and followed <sup>ⓘ</sup> him.
CSB17.15: While he was reclining at the table in Levi’s house, many tax collectors <sup>ⓙ</sup> and sinners <sup>ⓚ</sup> were eating <sup>[2]</sup> with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many who were following him.
CSB17.16: When the scribes <sup>ⓛ</sup> who were Pharisees <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup> saw that he was eating <sup>ⓝ</sup> with sinners <sup>ⓐ</sup> and tax collectors, <sup>ⓑ</sup> they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat <sup>[4]</sup> with tax collectors and sinners?”
CSB17.17: When Jesus heard this, he told them, “It is not those who are well who need <sup>ⓒ</sup> a doctor, <sup>ⓓ</sup> but those who are sick. <sup>ⓔ</sup> I didn’t come to call the righteous, <sup>ⓕ</sup> but sinners.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.18: Now <sup>ⓗ</sup> John’s <sup>ⓘ</sup> disciples <sup>ⓙ</sup> and the Pharisees <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup> were fasting. <sup>ⓛ</sup> People came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the Pharisees’ disciples fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
CSB17.19: Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot fast while the groom <sup>ⓜ</sup> is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cannot fast.
CSB17.20: But the time <sup>[6]</sup>will come <sup>ⓝ</sup>when the groom will be taken away from them, <sup>ⓐ</sup>and then they will fast <sup>ⓑ</sup>on that day.
CSB17.21: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. Otherwise, the new patch pulls away from the old cloth, <sup>ⓒ</sup> and a worse tear is made.
CSB17.22: And no one puts new wine <sup>ⓓ</sup> into old wineskins. Otherwise, the wine will burst <sup>ⓔ</sup> the skins, and the wine is lost as well as the skins. No, new wine is put into fresh wineskins.”
CSB17.23: On <sup>ⓕ</sup> the Sabbath he was going through the grainfields, and his disciples <sup>ⓖ</sup> began to make their way, picking some heads of grain.
CSB17.24: The Pharisees <sup>ⓗ</sup> said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is not lawful <sup>ⓘ</sup> on the Sabbath?”
CSB17.25: He said to them, “Have you never read what David <sup>ⓙ</sup> and those who were with him did when he was in need <sup>ⓚ</sup> and hungry <sup>ⓛ</sup>  —
CSB17.26: how he entered the house of God in the time of Abiathar <sup>ⓜ</sup> the high priest <sup>ⓝ</sup> and ate the bread of the Presence <sup>ⓐ</sup>  — which is not lawful for anyone to eat except the priests <sup>ⓑ</sup>  — and also gave some to his companions?”
CSB17.27: Then he told them, “The Sabbath was made for <sup>[7]</sup>man and not man for the Sabbath. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.28: So then, the Son of Man <sup>ⓓ</sup> is Lord <sup>ⓔ</sup> even of the Sabbath.” <sup>ⓕ</sup>

AMP.1: Jesus returned to Capernaum, and a few days later the news went out that He was at home.
AMP.2: So many people gathered together that there was no longer room [for them], not even near the door; and Jesus was discussing with them the word [of God].
AMP.3: Then they came, bringing to Him a paralyzed man, who was being carried by four men.
AMP.4: When they were unable to get to Him because of the crowd, they removed the roof above Jesus; and when they had dug out an opening, they let down the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
AMP.5: When Jesus saw their [active] faith [springing from confidence in Him], He said to the paralyzed man, "Son, your sins are forgiven."
AMP.6: But some of the scribes were sitting there debating in their hearts [the implication of what He had said],
AMP.7: "Why does this man talk that way? He is blaspheming; who can forgive sins [remove guilt, nullify sin's penalty, and assign righteousness] except God alone?"
AMP.8: Immediately Jesus, being fully aware [of their hostility] and knowing in His spirit that they were thinking this, said to them, "Why are you debating and arguing about these things in your hearts?
AMP.9: Which is easier, to say to the paralyzed man, 'Your sins are forgiven'; or to say, 'Get up, and pick up your mat and walk'?
AMP.10: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has the authority and power on earth to forgive sins"—He said to the paralyzed man,
AMP.11: "I say to you, get up, pick up your mat and go home."
AMP.12: And he got up and immediately picked up the mat and went out before them all, so that they all were astonished and they glorified and praised God, saying, "We have never seen anything like this!"
AMP.13: Jesus went out again along the [Galilean] seashore; and all the people were coming to Him, and He was teaching them.
AMP.14: As He was passing by, He saw Levi (Matthew) the son of Alphaeus sitting in the tax collector's booth, and He said to him, "Follow Me [as My disciple, accepting Me as your Master and Teacher and walking the same path of life that I walk]." And he got up and followed Him [becoming His disciple, believing and trusting in Him and following His example].
AMP.15: And it happened that Jesus was reclining at the table in Levi's house, and many tax collectors and sinners [including non-observant Jews] were eating with Him and His disciples; for there were many of them and they were following Him.
AMP.16: When the scribes [belonging to the sect] of the Pharisees saw that Jesus was eating with the sinners [including non-observant Jews] and tax collectors, they asked His disciples, "Why does He eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?"
AMP.17: When Jesus heard this, He said to them, "Those who are healthy have no need of a physician, but [only] those who are sick; I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners [who recognize their sin and humbly seek forgiveness]."
AMP.18: Now John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting [as a ritual]; and they came and asked Jesus, "Why are John's disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fasting, but Your disciples are not doing so?"
AMP.19: Jesus answered, "The attendants of the bridegroom cannot fast while the bridegroom is [still] with them, can they? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
AMP.20: But the days will come when the bridegroom is [forcefully] taken away from them, and they will fast at that time.
AMP.21: "No one sews a patch of unshrunk (new) cloth on an old garment; otherwise the patch pulls away from it, the new from the old, and the tear becomes worse.
AMP.22: No one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the [fermenting] wine will [expand and] burst the skins, and the wine is lost as well as the wineskins. But new wine must be put into new wineskins."
AMP.23: One Sabbath He was walking along [with His disciples] through the grainfields, and as they went along, His disciples began picking the heads of grain.
AMP.24: The Pharisees said to Him, "Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?"
AMP.25: Jesus said to them, "Have you never read [in the Scriptures] what David did when he was in need and was hungry, he and his companions;
AMP.26: how he went into the house of God in the time of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the sacred bread, which is not lawful for anyone but the priests to eat, and how he also gave it to the men who were with him?"
AMP.27: Jesus said to them, "The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
AMP.28: So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath [and He has authority over it]."

MSG.1: After a few days, Jesus returned to Capernaum, and word got around that he was back home.
MSG.2: A crowd gathered, jamming the entrance so no one could get in or out. He was teaching the Word.
MSG.3: They brought a paraplegic to him, carried by four men.
MSG.4: When they weren't able to get in because of the crowd, they removed part of the roof and lowered the paraplegic on his stretcher.
MSG.5: Impressed by their bold belief, Jesus said to the paraplegic, "Son, I forgive your sins."
MSG.6: Some religion scholars sitting there started whispering among themselves,
MSG.7: "He can't talk that way! That's blasphemy! God and only God can forgive sins."
MSG.8: Jesus knew right away what they were thinking, and said, "Why are you so skeptical?
MSG.9: Which is simpler: to say to the paraplegic, 'I forgive your sins,' or say, 'Get up, take your stretcher, and start walking'?
MSG.10: Well, just so it's clear that I'm the Son of Man and authorized to do either, or both..." (he looked now at the paraplegic),
MSG.11: "Get up. Pick up your stretcher and go home."
MSG.12: And the man did it—got up, grabbed his stretcher, and walked out, with everyone there watching him. They rubbed their eyes, incredulous—and then praised God, saying, "We've never seen anything like this!"
MSG.13: Then Jesus went again to walk alongside the lake. Again a crowd came to him, and he taught them.
MSG.14: Strolling along, he saw Levi, son of Alphaeus, at his work collecting taxes. Jesus said, "Come along with me." He came.
MSG.15: Later Jesus and his disciples were at home having supper with a collection of disreputable guests. Unlikely as it seems, more than a few of them had become followers.
MSG.16: The religion scholars and Pharisees saw him keeping this kind of company and lit into his disciples: "What kind of example is this, acting cozy with the riff-raff?"
MSG.17: Jesus, overhearing, shot back, "Who needs a doctor: the healthy or the sick? I'm here inviting the sin-sick, not the spiritually-fit."
MSG.18: The disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees made a practice of fasting. Some people confronted Jesus: "Why do the followers of John and the Pharisees take on the discipline of fasting, but your followers don't?"
MSG.19: Jesus said, "When you're celebrating a wedding, you don't skimp on the cake and wine. You feast. Later you may need to pull in your belt, but not now. As long as the bride and groom are with you, you have a good time. No one throws cold water on a friendly bonfire. This is Kingdom Come!"
MSG.21: He went on, "No one cuts up a fine silk scarf to patch old work clothes; you want fabrics that match.
MSG.22: And you don't put your wine in cracked bottles."
MSG.23: One Sabbath day he was walking through a field of ripe grain.
MSG.24: As his disciples made a path, they pulled off heads of grain. The Pharisees told on them to Jesus: "Look, your disciples are breaking Sabbath rules!"
MSG.25: Jesus said, "Really? Haven't you ever read what David did when he was hungry, along with those who were with him?
MSG.26: How he entered the sanctuary and ate fresh bread off the altar, with the Chief Priest Abiathar right there watching—holy bread that no one but priests were allowed to eat—and handed it out to his companions?"
MSG.27: Then Jesus said, "The Sabbath was made to serve us; we weren't made to serve the Sabbath.
MSG.28: The Son of Man is no lackey to the Sabbath. He's in charge!"

NIV.1:
Jesus Heals a Paralytic
A few days later, when Jesus again entered Capernaum, the people heard that he had come home.
NIV.2: So many gathered that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them.
NIV.3: Some men came, bringing to him a paralytic, carried by four of them.
NIV.4: Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus and, after digging through it, lowered the mat the paralyzed man was lying on.
NIV.5: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
NIV.6: Now some teachers of the law were sitting there, thinking to themselves,
NIV.7: “Why does this fellow talk like that? He’s blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
NIV.8: Immediately Jesus knew in his spirit that this was what they were thinking in their hearts, and he said to them, “Why are you thinking these things?
NIV.9: Which is easier: to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat and walk’?
NIV.10: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins…” He said to the paralytic,
NIV.11: “I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.”
NIV.12: He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
NIV.13:
The Calling of Levi
Once again Jesus went out beside the lake. A large crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
NIV.14: As he walked along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax collector’s booth. “Follow me,” Jesus told him, and Levi got up and followed him.
NIV.15: While Jesus was having dinner at Levi’s house, many tax collectors and “sinners” were eating with him and his disciples, for there were many who followed him.
NIV.16: When the teachers of the law who were Pharisees saw him eating with the “sinners” and tax collectors, they asked his disciples: “Why does he eat with tax collectors and ‘sinners’?”
NIV.17: On hearing this, Jesus said to them, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick. I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”
NIV.18:
Jesus Questioned About Fasting
Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. Some people came and asked Jesus, “How is it that John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees are fasting, but yours are not?”
NIV.19: Jesus answered, “How can the guests of the bridegroom fast while he is with them? They cannot, so long as they have him with them.
NIV.20: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them, and on that day they will fast.
NIV.21: “No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. If he does, the new piece will pull away from the old, making the tear worse.
NIV.22: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the wineskins will be ruined. No, he pours new wine into new wineskins.”
NIV.23:
Lord of the Sabbath
One Sabbath Jesus was going through the grainfields, and as his disciples walked along, they began to pick some heads of grain.
NIV.24: The Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?”
NIV.25: He answered, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need?
NIV.26: In the days of Abiathar the high priest, he entered the house of God and ate the consecrated bread, which is lawful only for priests to eat. And he also gave some to his companions.”
NIV.27: Then he said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
NIV.28: So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.”

NRSVCE.1: When he returned to Capernaum after some days, it was reported that he was at home.
NRSVCE.2: So many gathered around that there was no longer room for them, not even in front of the door; and he was speaking the word to them.
NRSVCE.3: Then some people came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them.
NRSVCE.4: And when they could not bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, they removed the roof above him; and after having dug through it, they let down the mat on which the paralytic lay.
NRSVCE.5: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
NRSVCE.6: Now some of the scribes were sitting there, questioning in their hearts,
NRSVCE.7: “Why does this fellow speak in this way? It is blasphemy! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
NRSVCE.8: At once Jesus perceived in his spirit that they were discussing these questions among themselves; and he said to them, “Why do you raise such questions in your hearts?
NRSVCE.9: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Stand up and take your mat and walk’?
NRSVCE.10: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he said to the paralytic —
NRSVCE.11: “I say to you, stand up, take your mat and go to your home.”
NRSVCE.12: And he stood up, and immediately took the mat and went out before all of them; so that they were all amazed and glorified God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
NRSVCE.13: Jesus went out again beside the sea; the whole crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
NRSVCE.14: As he was walking along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
NRSVCE.15: And as he sat at dinner in Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were also sitting with Jesus and his disciples — for there were many who followed him.
NRSVCE.16: When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they said to his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
NRSVCE.17: When Jesus heard this, he said to them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick; I have come to call not the righteous but sinners.”
NRSVCE.18: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting; and people came and said to him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
NRSVCE.19: Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot fast while the bridegroom is with them, can they? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
NRSVCE.20: The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast on that day.
NRSVCE.21: “No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak; otherwise, the patch pulls away from it, the new from the old, and a worse tear is made.
NRSVCE.22: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and so are the skins; but one puts new wine into fresh wineskins.”
NRSVCE.23: One sabbath he was going through the grainfields; and as they made their way his disciples began to pluck heads of grain.
NRSVCE.24: The Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?”
NRSVCE.25: And he said to them, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need of food?
NRSVCE.26: He entered the house of God, when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and he gave some to his companions.”
NRSVCE.27: Then he said to them, “The sabbath was made for humankind, and not humankind for the sabbath;
NRSVCE.28: so the Son of Man is lord even of the sabbath.”

NET.1: Now after some days, when he returned to Capernaum, the news spread that he was at home.
NET.2: So many gathered that there was no longer any room, not even by the door, and he preached the word to them.
NET.3: Some people came bringing to him a paralytic, carried by four of them.
NET.4: When they were not able to bring him in because of the crowd, they removed the roof above Jesus. Then, after tearing it out, they lowered the stretcher the paralytic was lying on.
NET.5: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
NET.6: Now some of the experts in the law were sitting there, turning these things over in their minds:
NET.7: “Why does this man speak this way? He is blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
NET.8: Now immediately, when Jesus realized in his spirit that they were contemplating such thoughts, he said to them, “Why are you thinking such things in your hearts?
NET.9: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Stand up, take your stretcher, and walk’?
NET.10: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins,” — he said to the paralytic —
NET.11: “I tell you, stand up, take your stretcher, and go home.”
NET.12: And immediately the man stood up, took his stretcher, and went out in front of them all. They were all amazed and glorified God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
NET.13: Jesus went out again by the sea. The whole crowd came to him, and he taught them.
NET.14: As he went along, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax booth. “Follow me,” he said to him. And he got up and followed him.
NET.15: As Jesus was having a meal in Levi’s home, many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many who followed him.
NET.16: When the experts in the law and the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they said to his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
NET.17: When Jesus heard this he said to them, “Those who are healthy don’t need a physician, but those who are sick do. I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”
NET.18: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. So they came to Jesus and said, “Why do the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples don’t fast?”
NET.19: Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot fast while the bridegroom is with them, can they? As long as they have the bridegroom with them they do not fast.
NET.20: But the days are coming when the bridegroom will be taken from them, and at that time they will fast.
NET.21: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; otherwise, the patch pulls away from it, the new from the old, and the tear becomes worse.
NET.22: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins; otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the skins will be destroyed. Instead new wine is poured into new wineskins.”
NET.23: Jesus was going through the grain fields on a Sabbath, and his disciples began to pick some heads of wheat as they made their way.
NET.24: So the Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is against the law on the Sabbath?”
NET.25: He said to them, “Have you never read what David did when he was in need and he and his companions were hungry —
NET.26: how he entered the house of God when Abiathar was high priest and ate the sacred bread, which is against the law for any but the priests to eat, and also gave it to his companions?”
NET.27: Then he said to them, “The Sabbath was made for people, not people for the Sabbath.
NET.28: For this reason the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”
